pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 14-46 creates protocol file at this exact path

Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 94 creates asset with Code='AST-PUMP-SUMP'

Code analysis: verifier.py line 43 includes 'AST-ARCH-B01' in relocate_targets list; asset is Documents type (setup_task.sh line 86)
Code analysis: verifier.py line 57 includes 'AST-PUMP-MAIN' in protect_targets list; asset is Mechanical type (setup_task.sh line 91)

Code analysis: verifier.py line 57 includes 'AST-PUMP-SUMP' in protect_targets list; special sump pump trap check at lines 77-92 confirms [PROTECT] requirement despite being mechanical; protocol explicitly states sump pump is critical exception (setup_task.sh lines 31-33)
Code analysis: verifier.py line 43 includes 'AST-UPS-B01' in relocate_targets list; asset is Electronics type (setup_task.sh line 101)
Code analysis: verifier.py lines 70-75 check that furniture (AST-DESK-B01) should NOT be tagged; protocol states 'DO NOT TAG' for furniture (setup_task.sh lines 35-37)

privileged_info_summary: Emergency flood response asset triage task uses a controlled, synthetic dataset created during setup. Task requires tagging six specific assets by type: three electronics items (Server AST-SRV-B01, Archives AST-ARCH-B01, UPS AST-UPS-B01) must be tagged [RELOCATE]; two mechanical items (Main Pump AST-PUMP-MAIN, Sump Pump AST-PUMP-SUMP) must be tagged [PROTECT]; one furniture item (Desk AST-DESK-B01) must not be tagged. Assets are located in three vulnerable basement rooms (B-101 Server/Archive, B-102 Mechanical, B-103 UPS/Power). The sump pump is a critical trap—despite being mechanical equipment, it must be protected in-place, not relocated. Scoring: relocate tags 30 points (10 each), protect tags 20 points (10 each), furniture handling 15 points, sump pump exception 15 points, work order creation 20 points; 70+ points required to pass.
